seo.title: Conway vs Benton: Which Little Rock Suburb Is Right for You? | Metro Little Rock
seo.description: Conway vs Benton — comparing these popular Little Rock metro suburbs on schools, price, lifestyle, commute, and growth to help you decide where to plant roots.

intro: Conway and Benton are the two most popular suburbs outside Little Rock proper — Conway to the north on I-40, Benton to the southwest on I-30. Both have strong schools, growing amenities, and value home prices. But they have very different personalities.

metrics.notes: Benton offers slightly better value on a per-square-foot basis in most price ranges.
Both school systems are strong. Conway has more school choice within the district due to city size.
Benton is closer and I-30 typically has less congestion than I-40 north of the river.

metrics.b_value: Traditional small city — Saline County seat
metrics.notes: Conway has a university-town energy and more dining/entertainment options. Benton is quieter and more traditional.
Conway has meaningfully more independent restaurants and dining options due to university population.
Conway has its own economic engine. Benton's growth is more closely tied to Little Rock and Bryant.
Conway's trail system is more developed and better connected.
Both have active homebuilding. Conway has more variety at different price points.

summary: Choose Conway if you want a city with its own identity, more restaurant and entertainment options, a university-town energy, and don't mind the longer drive on I-40. Choose Benton if you want a shorter commute to Little Rock, a quieter traditional town feel, slightly lower home prices, and proximity to the Saline County lifestyle. Both are excellent choices — the commute direction often determines which makes more sense for your specific employer location.
